name: build-auditor
description: Audits the build output of the project to ensure it meets Deerflow production-readiness standards. Validates bundle size constraints, verifies the presence of required output files (HTML entry points, JS bundles, CSS assets, source maps), checks for proper tree-shaking (unused exports), and ensures the build artifacts are optimized for deployment.

buildPath.description: Path to the build output directory to audit (e.g., './dist', './out', './build').

minSizeKB.description: Minimum expected total bundle size in kilobytes. Useful for detecting broken builds that produce suspiciously small output.

maxSizeKB.description: Maximum allowed total bundle size in kilobytes. Build fails if the total output exceeds this threshold.

requiredFiles.description: List of file patterns that must exist in the build output for the build to be considered valid.

pattern.description: Glob pattern or exact file path relative to buildPath

minCount.description: Minimum number of files matching this pattern

maxSizeKB.description: Maximum size in KB for any single file matching this pattern

checkSourceMaps.description: Whether to verify that source map files (.map) exist alongside JS bundles for debugging in production.

checkTreeShaking.description: Whether to analyze bundles for evidence of unused code (e.g., detecting known library exports that should have been tree-shaken away).

checkCompression.description: Whether to verify that assets have been minified (variable names shortened, whitespace removed) and optionally check gzip/brotli compression ratios.

framework.description: Build framework being used. Enables framework-specific audit rules.

topFiles.description: Top 10 largest files in the build output
